title: create_app() always provisions the persistent instance/books.db, even under test
evidence: app.py:19-24 reads app.config["TESTING"] at factory time (default False), so SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI is set to sqlite:///books.db and db.create_all() at app.py:28-29 creates instance/books.db; test fixtures (tests/test_acceptance.py:26-27, tests/test_unit.py:23-24) override the URI to sqlite:// only AFTER create_app() returns. An empty instance/books.db (0 rows) is left as a side-effect.
suggestion: Accept a config/testing flag as a create_app(testing=False) argument (or read an env var) and choose the URI before init_app/create_all, rather than relying on TESTING being set post-construction.

title: ATDD acceptance tests use a back-door DB reset in setup
evidence: tests/test_acceptance.py:16 imports get_db, db as _db_module and the autouse fixture at tests/test_acceptance.py:38-44 calls _db_module.drop_all()/create_all() directly; the ATDD prompt asks for 'no back-door access to internals or the database'. Assertions themselves remain black-box over the HTTP surface.
suggestion: Reset state through the public interface (e.g. a test-only teardown that DELETEs created books) or spin up a fresh in-memory app per test without importing the db module.

title: README undercounts the unit tests
evidence: README.md:100 states unit tests are '5 tests' but tests/test_unit.py defines 8 (grep def test_ = 8); total suite is 35 not 32.
suggestion: Update the README test summary to '8 unit tests / 35 total'.

title: Book.to_dict() defined but unused by route handlers
evidence: app.py:177-184 defines to_dict(), but every route (app.py:66-72, 85-94, 102-108, 142-148) hand-rolls the same serialization dict inline.
suggestion: Serialize responses via book.to_dict() to remove the duplicated field lists.
